title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · Relational Recall
summary: A team must move a browser prototype toward PostgreSQL, justify any non-relational component, and defend the complete data system. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
Choose relational, document, key-value, graph, or mixed models from access, integrity, evolution, and consistency needs.
Explain replication, partitioning, distributed transactions, consistency choices, and failure boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Dialect Portability · changed case · Review Dialect Portability
steps.instruction:
review case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 review artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 6; justify any non-relational component.

Which action demonstrates Dialect Portability without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Dialect Portability · changed case · Review Dialect Portability.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate standard relational intent from sqlite and postgresql syntax, typing, null, date, and feature behavior. For Dialect Portability · changed case · Review Dialect Portability, keep Dialect Portability connected to executable relational evidence.

stimulus.title: Capstone Defense case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 review artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 2; justify any non-relational component.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Passing the original demo proves the database system is production ready.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence. Artifact under review: a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Run a convenient Capstone Defense query for separate evidence from confidence, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid.
The record names the assumption, applies Capstone Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Capstone Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Capstone Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 review artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 3; justify any non-relational component.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Capstone Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Capstone Defense, keep Capstone Defense connected to executable relational evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.
